finding.summary: Severe negative stereo correlation (-0.42) indicates significant out-of-phase content, which will cause cancellation in mono and may reduce impact.
finding.probable_cause: Excessive stereo widening or phase inversion in the source or processing; the side-channel level (-15.0 dB RMS) dominates the mid-channel (-27.0 dB RMS) by 12 dB, producing an anti-phased stereo field.
finding.confidence_reason: Track isolation is verified, silence_fraction is low (0.05), and the correlation value is far from normal (0 to +1), making the diagnosis robust.

evidence_refs.description: Measured stereo correlation of -0.42, indicating out-of-phase content.
Side RMS level is -15.0 dB, 12 dB higher than mid, causing negative correlation.

proposal.reason: No FX chain is available to correct the stereo correlation; the track requires a mono-compatible stereo widener or polarity alignment plugin before further processing.
